name: Rubio's Challah
recipeInstructions:
In a small bowl, place warm water and yeast until softened. In a large bowl, combine sugar, salt, salad oil, 3 eggs slightly beaten, and about 3-4 cups of flour. Mix with the yeast mixture, adding more flour if needed to make a workable dough. Knead until smooth and elastic. Do not underknead. Place in an oiled bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled in size. Punch down the dough and divide it into two halves. Cut each half into three equal parts.
Roll each part into a strip that is 15 inches long. Place the strips on a greased baking sheet. Braid the strips, fastening them at each end. Repeat with the second half of the dough. Beat the remaining egg slightly and brush the tops and sides of the dough. Sprinkle with sesame seeds. Bake in a preheated oven set to 325 degrees for 30-40 minutes.
